questions.text: Which statement can be used to select one of many alternatives?
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: An if statement is used to execute code conditionally based on a boolean expression. It allows you to choose between two different paths of execution. An if statement is only for two alternatives, whereas a switch statement can handle multiple options.

A loop statement allows you to execute a block of code repeatedly until a certain condition is met. It's useful for iterating over a sequence of elements or performing a task multiple times. A loop is for repeated execution, not for selecting a single alternative.

A switch statement is a multi-way branching statement that allows you to select one of many code blocks to execute based on the value of a variable. It's particularly useful when you have multiple possible conditions and want to execute different code for each one. In this case, a switch statement is the most suitable choice for selecting one of many alternatives based on the value of a variable.

A label is a named marker in your code that you can jump to using a goto statement (which is generally considered bad practice in modern programming). Labels themselves don't directly select alternatives. Labels don't directly achieve selection; they're used for jumping to specific parts of the code.
